steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The histogram shows 14 salmon weights. A 15th salmon weighing 85 pounds is added. The question asks which of two statements — (I) the median increases, (II) the mean increases — must be true.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Statement I — median must increase: With 14 values the original median is the average of the 7th and 8th values. Counting from the histogram: positions 1–3 are in [10, 15), positions 4–9 are in [15, 20). Both the 7th and 8th values lie in [15, 20). With 15 values, the new median is just the 8th value — still in [15, 20). If the 7th and 8th values happen to be equal, the new median equals the old one. Statement I is not guaranteed. ✗
  • Statement II — mean must increase: Let $S$ be the original sum of 14 weights. The new mean exceeds the old mean when $\dfrac{S+85}{15} > \dfrac{S}{14}$. Cross-multiplying: $14(S+85) > 15S \Rightarrow S < 1{,}190$. The maximum possible $S$ from the histogram is $3(15)+6(20)+2(25)+1(30)+2(35)=315$. Since $315 < 1{,}190$ always holds, the mean always increases. Statement II is guaranteed. ✓
Only Statement II must be true — that matches choice B.
